message:
feat(wasm): add browser edition cfg gates across networking, config, and runtime modules

Gate platform-specific APIs (raw TCP sockets, socket2, libc, DNS lookup,
timeouts) behind `#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]` so the crate compiles
cleanly for wasm32-unknown-unknown targets. This is a foundational step toward
the Browser Edition, where raw socket and OS-level networking primitives are
unavailable.

Key changes:
- src/config.rs: Gate ServerConfig, ServerProfile, SocketAddr, and HTTP
  listener/pool config types. Move native-only tests into a cfg-gated submodule
  so wasm32 builds skip tests that depend on SocketAddr or ServerConfig.
- src/cx/cx.rs: Gate the CxRuntime::block_on implementation that calls the
  native reactor directly, unreachable in browser contexts.
- src/net/mod.rs: Add browser_tcp_unsupported() helper for consistent error
  messages when TCP operations are attempted in wasm32 builds.
- src/net/tcp/{listener,mod,socket,split,stream,traits}.rs: Provide wasm32
  fallback stubs returning io::ErrorKind::Unsupported for all raw TCP operations
  (connect, bind, accept, split, poll_read/write, setsockopt, nodelay, etc.).
  Native code paths are preserved behind cfg(not(wasm32)).
- src/net/udp.rs: Same pattern for UDP — gate socket2 and OS-level socket
  operations, add browser fallback stubs.
- src/runtime/{mod,reactor/mod,reactor/source}.rs: Gate reactor source
  registration and OS I/O driver hooks; add wasm32 no-op stubs.

No behavioral changes on native targets. All existing tests continue to pass
unmodified on x86_64/aarch64.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
